records.ety: [F. obdience, L. obedientia, oboedientia. See Obedient, and cf. Obeisance.]

defs: The act of obeying, or the state of being obedient; compliance with that which is required by authority; subjection to rightful restraint or control.
quotes: Government must compel the obedience of individuals.

defs: Words or actions denoting submission to authority; dutifulness.
A following; a body of adherents; as, the Roman Catholic obedience, or the whole body of persons who submit to the authority of the pope.
A cell (or offshoot of a larger monastery) governed by a prior.
One of the three monastic vows.
The written precept of a superior in a religious order or congregation to a subject.
